what_it_does: Declares one seed AI-tic, 'closure_form', as a module-level AI_TIC dict. Defines its id, label 'closure-form', a list of six text markers (in conclusion, to summarize, in summary, to wrap up, final thoughts, that's everything), and a description identifying closure-form writing (summarized/finished, expansion-ratio<1) as a named Tim frustration that kills institutional memory.
observations: Pure declarative data file — no functions, no imports, only the AI_TIC dict
id ('closure_form') equals the file stem as required by the registry schema
markers is a non-empty list of six phrase strings
Docstring cross-references runtime/ai_tics.py and ai_tics/AGENTS.md and names the open-future-writing frustration
standouts: Encodes a behavioral writing antipattern as matchable marker phrases — the 'cues the fingerprint pass matches'
conventions: Conforms exactly to the AI_TIC schema in ai_tics/AGENTS.md (id == stem, non-empty markers, optional label/desc)
Follows the file-per-tic self-registering registry pattern (add-a-file, no code branch)
notes: A single seed AI-tic file cataloguing closure-form phrasing as a generic-AI tell to subtract.
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — docstring says it is the closure-form seed tic and the AI_TIC dict declares exactly that.
intention_signals: To register closure-form writing as a detectable generic-AI tell so the cognition fingerprint pass can flag and subtract it, defending institutional memory across sessions.
novelty: Operationalizes a soft writing-style frustration into concrete matchable phrases as registry data.
contribution: Contributes the closure_form tic entry and its six marker phrases to the AI-tic catalogue.
summary_for_embedding: An ai_tics seed file declaring the closure_form AI-tic with marker phrases like 'in conclusion' and 'to summarize'.
intention_for_embedding: To catalogue closure-form writing as a generic-AI tell the cognition fingerprint pass detects and subtracts.
